title: fagraea tacapala subsp. gracilis
intro: Fagraea tacapala subsp. gracilis, also known as Tacana flower or Tacapala, is a plant species that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Mexican herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fagraea tacapala subsp. gracilis include the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Fagraea tacapala subsp. gracilis is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
